title: Radio Program Manifest
description: Canonical published JSON manifest for a radio program (Build Spec sections 12, 16). A program is a named show (e.g. 'Morning Awakening with Eliana') hosted by an artist persona, scheduled on one station via a cron expression, with a fixed duration. The Jubilee Radio Engine v3.0 reads these manifests from /radio/programs/{id}.json to build station lineups.

$schema.description: Optional self-link to this schema document.

name.description: Display name of the program (e.g. 'Morning Awakening with Eliana').

description.description: Optional editorial description shown on program landing pages and electronic program guides.

host_artist_id.description: Reference to catalog.artists.id of the hosting persona. Omit for unhosted programs (automated rotations).

station_id.description: Reference to radio.stations.id of the airing station.

schedule_cron.description: Cron expression describing the recurring airing schedule, in standard 5-field form (minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week) with an optional 6th field for year. Times are interpreted in the station's local timezone.

duration_min.description: Program duration in minutes. Capped at 24 hours.

playlist_id.description: Optional reference to a playlist that backs this program. When present, the radio engine plays the playlist contents during the program's airtime.

is_active.description: Whether the program is currently in rotation. Inactive programs remain in the manifest for historical / audit purposes but are skipped by the radio engine.

cover_art_url.description: Optional cover art for the program (used in EPG and station landing pages).

timezone.description: IANA timezone name (e.g. 'America/Chicago', 'Europe/Bucharest') that schedule_cron is interpreted in. Defaults to the station's configured timezone when omitted.
